description:
Annual health check-up is a benefit provided under several health insurance plans where the insured becomes eligible for a free medical check-up every year. 

In this video, we have discussed various Terms & Conditions in Annual Health Check Up & How you can avail these free medical check ups.

We have also compared Annual Health Check ups offered  by different Health Insurance Companies.

WATCH THE VIDEO FOR MORE DETAILS :)
✅ To buy insurance policies, click here - https://hareepatti.com/contact-us/

For any queries:

comments.text:
To request your Complimentary Annual Health Checkup, please follow the simple steps:

1. Visit - https://rules.nivabupa.com/customer-service/
2. Click on Policy Related and then Annual Health Check up Request
3. A new page will open, enter your Registered Mobile number and validate with OTP
4. Select your policy and submit the request
5. Niva Bupa representative will get in touch with you within 48 hours to schedule your tests

comments.text:
Hello ma'am,
I had purchased an insurance from HDFC Ergo last month for my father. But the company issued the insurance on my name instead and charging me the amount for my fathers age. After multiple emails and calls, they told me that they'll correct the insurance papers and will provide me the updated policy within 24 to 48 hrs. But instead they mailed me and asked for all the documents and additional documents again. I am totally fed up. I purchased the policy on EMI, so the company is cutting the money every month but no resolution has been provided. Could you please tell me what to do in this scenario? It feels like someone looted my hard earned money. Can you please help here?

comments.text:
Hello Gurleen. Thanks for the information. I have some doubts:

Consider this as a situation. A person has ported an old policy to niva bupa last year and that time he was not aware of any pre existing diseases. In general health checkup, if BP/ Diabetes (slow growing disease) are diagnosed,
1. Is it necessary/recommended to declare newly diagnosed disease before renewal of the policy this year?
2. Are there any repercussions around increases in premium?
3. Will this affect sum insured for any/ newly diagnosed disease?

It would be great to have answers for these questions.
comments.author: @rajatwason

comments.text:
It is always recommended to maintain your medical file with all medical infromation documents like test reports/ Doctor's precriptions
 In your case, since it was not a PED, you are not obligated to inform insurance comapny. But , yes maintain a file which will come handy at the time of claims - For eg- company may ask for first consultatyion paper/ diagnosis papers for BP/ Diabetes
